text: What if I told you there's a giant cyclone of cold air up there, influencing our weather? It's called the polar vortex, and it exists high in the stratosphere. When stable, it keeps the cold air contained. But disruption? That's when chaos happens. Just a slight shift can send freezing temperatures spiraling southward. Sometimes it leads to extreme winter weather, but don’t panic—it's a natural cycle. Curious about its effects? Scientists are still researching this complex phenomenon. Want to learn more about weather mysteries? Follow for more!
